docs.title: Proxmox2NetBox
docs.text: Proxmox2NetBox is a NetBox v4 plugin that synchronizes Proxmox inventory data into NetBox.

docs.title: Runtime Scope
docs.text:
This project is plugin-only:

  • No external FastAPI service is required.
  • No separate backend deployment is required.
  • Sync runs directly from the NetBox plugin UI and NetBox jobs.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text:
• NetBox: >=4.2.0, <5.0.0
  • Python: >=3.8
  • Proxmox VE: >=8.x (tested baseline)

docs.title: What Sync Covers
docs.text:
• Proxmox nodes → NetBox dcim.Device
  • Proxmox QEMU/LXC VMs → NetBox virtualization.VirtualMachine
  • VM interfaces → NetBox virtualization.VMInterface with per-interface IP assignment
  • QEMU guest-agent IPs as fallback when static VM config has no IPs (MAC-based interface matching)
  • Stale IPs removed when no longer present in Proxmox config
  • Endpoint-level Site and VRF mapping (devices/IPs placed in the configured NetBox Site/VRF)
  • Per-endpoint Node Device Type (real hardware model, e.g. Dell PowerEdge R740)
  • Per-node Device Type Mapping for individual node overrides
  • Endpoint metadata refresh (mode, version, repoid, cluster name)
  • Sync process tracking (SyncProcess)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Install the package in NetBox venv: pip install proxmox2netbox
  2. Enable plugin in NetBox config: PLUGINS = ["proxmox2netbox"]
  3. Run migrations/static collection.
  4. Add at least one Proxmox endpoint in plugin UI.
  5. Run sync from plugin home page or queue the NetBox job.

docs.title: Installation Guides
docs.text:
• Installing using pip
  • Installing using git
  • Installing using Docker

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
• Automatically remove nodes in NetBox when removed in Proxmox (aligned with VM cleanup behavior)
  • Add per-object sync actions for individual nodes/VMs
  • Improve periodic sync scheduling through NetBox jobs
  • Improve documentation and test coverage for sync mapping
  • Extend multi-cluster support

Track requests and bugs in GitHub Issues.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Proxmox2NetBox is configured through the NetBox UI. No configuration file is required.

docs.title: Proxmox Endpoint
docs.text:
Create at least one endpoint under:

Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Endpoints → Proxmox Endpoints

docs.title: Required fields
docs.text: Field Description name Display name for this endpoint domain and/or ip_address Proxmox host address username Proxmox user (e.g. root@pam) password or token_name + token_value Authentication credentials

docs.title: Optional fields
docs.text: Field Description port Proxmox API port (default: 8006) verify_ssl TLS certificate verification (default: enabled) netbox_site NetBox Site to place synced nodes and VMs in netbox_vrf NetBox VRF to assign synced IP addresses to netbox_device_type NetBox DeviceType for synced Proxmox nodes (e.g. Dell PowerEdge R740)

docs.title: Per-Node Device Type Mapping
docs.text:
For individual node hardware overrides, use:

Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Configuration → Node Type Mappings

Map a specific node hostname (e.g. pve01) to a specific NetBox DeviceType. Nodes without an explicit mapping use the endpoint-level device type, or the generic Proxmox Node fallback.

See Required Parameters for the minimum setup before first sync.

docs.title: Required Parameters
docs.text: The following fields are required on at least one Proxmox Endpoint before sync will work.

docs.title: Proxmox Endpoint — required fields
docs.text:
Field Example Notes name My Proxmox Cluster Arbitrary display name domain proxmox.example.com Hostname or FQDN. At least one of domain/ip_address must be set. ip_address 192.168.1.10 IP address. Can be used instead of or together with domain. username root@pam Proxmox user in user@realm format password s3cret Password auth. Required unless using API token. token_name mytoken Token auth. Required together with token_value if not using password. token_value xxxxxxxx-... Token value (UUID format)

Either password or both token_name + token_value must be provided.

docs.title: Minimum NetBox configuration
docs.text:
PLUGINS = ["proxmox2netbox"]

Migrations must be applied:

python manage.py migrate
python manage.py collectstatic --no-input

docs.title: Proxmox API
docs.text:
The plugin connects directly to the Proxmox VE REST API from the NetBox runtime.

Authentication modes: - Username + password (root@pam style) - Username + API token (token_name + token_value)

Endpoints used: - /api2/json/nodes — list cluster nodes - /api2/json/nodes/{node}/qemu — list QEMU VMs - /api2/json/nodes/{node}/lxc — list LXC containers - /api2/json/nodes/{node}/qemu/{vmid}/config — VM config (interfaces, IPs) - /api2/json/nodes/{node}/qemu/{vmid}/agent/network-get-interfaces — guest agent IPs (QEMU only) - /api2/json/cluster/status — cluster name and node membership

docs.title: NetBox REST API (plugin endpoints)
docs.text:
The plugin exposes its own models via NetBox's REST API under /api/plugins/proxmox2netbox/.

Available endpoints: - /api/plugins/proxmox2netbox/endpoints/ — ProxmoxEndpoint CRUD - /api/plugins/proxmox2netbox/sync-processes/ — SyncProcess read

The serializers expose all endpoint fields including netbox_site, netbox_vrf, and netbox_device_type.

docs.title: Background Jobs
docs.text: Proxmox2NetBox integrates with NetBox's built-in job system (RQ workers).

docs.title: Job class
docs.text: Proxmox2NetBoxSyncJob in proxmox2netbox/jobs.py is a JobRunner subclass registered as a NetBox system job.

docs.title: Triggering sync
docs.text:
From UI:

  • Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Full Update — runs full sync for all enabled endpoints

From NetBox shell:

from proxmox2netbox.jobs import Proxmox2NetBoxSyncJob
from proxmox2netbox.choices import SyncTypeChoices

Proxmox2NetBoxSyncJob.enqueue(sync_type=SyncTypeChoices.ALL)
Proxmox2NetBoxSyncJob.enqueue(sync_type=SyncTypeChoices.DEVICES)
Proxmox2NetBoxSyncJob.enqueue(sync_type=SyncTypeChoices.VIRTUAL_MACHINES)

docs.title: Job behavior
docs.text:
• The job class is an orchestrator only — all sync mapping and upsert logic lives in services/proxmox_sync.py.
  • Jobs run in NetBox RQ workers (netbox-rq service).
  • Job results are visible in Admin → Jobs in the NetBox UI.
  • If an RQ worker is restarted, make sure it picks up the latest installed package version.

docs.title: Backups & Proxmox Backup Server
docs.text: Not in scope. Proxmox2NetBox does not integrate with Proxmox Backup Server (PBS) and does not sync backup jobs, schedules, or datastore status to NetBox.

docs.title: Current scope
docs.text:
This plugin is a one-way inventory sync: Proxmox → NetBox DCIM/IPAM. It covers nodes, VMs, LXC containers, interfaces, and IP addresses.

Backup infrastructure (PBS datastores, backup jobs, retention policies) is not modelled in standard NetBox and is therefore out of scope.

docs.title: NetBox database backup
docs.text: For backing up the NetBox PostgreSQL database itself, refer to standard PostgreSQL backup tooling (pg_dump, pg_basebackup) — this is unrelated to this plugin.

docs.title: LXC Containers
docs.text: Proxmox LXC containers are synced to NetBox in the same way as QEMU VMs — as virtualization.VirtualMachine objects.

docs.title: What gets synced
docs.text: Proxmox field NetBox field Container name Virtual machine name Node name Cluster node Cluster name Cluster vCPU count vCPUs Memory (MB) Memory (MB) Container state Status (active / offline)

docs.title: Interfaces and IPs
docs.text:
LXC container network interfaces (net0, net1, …) are synced as VMInterface objects with MAC addresses.

IP assignment follows the same logic as for QEMU VMs: - Static IPs from container config - Stale IPs removed when no longer present in config

Note: LXC containers do not support the QEMU guest agent, so the guest-agent IP fallback is not available for containers.

docs.title: Sync process tracking
docs.text:
Every sync run creates a SyncProcess record in NetBox, visible via the plugin UI.

The record captures: - Start and end time - Endpoint synced - Status (success / error)

docs.title: Job results
docs.text: Background sync jobs are visible in NetBox under Admin → Jobs. The job result contains the sync summary and any errors encountered.

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
Sync operations log at INFO level. Errors log at ERROR or WARNING.

When running from the CLI:

python manage.py netbox_unifi_sync_run --dry-run --json

Log output goes to stdout and the configured Django log handlers.

docs.title: What is NOT monitored
docs.text:
• Real-time Proxmox node status (CPU, memory utilization) is not polled.
  • Proxmox alerts or HA events are not forwarded to NetBox.
  • The plugin does not send webhooks or notifications — use NetBox's built-in webhook system for that.

docs.title: Interface sync
docs.text:
Each VM/container network interface (net0, net1, …) is synced as a virtualization.VMInterface.

  • Interface name derived from Proxmox config key
  • MAC address synced as primary_mac_address (NetBox 4.5+ MACAddress model)

docs.title: IP address sync
docs.text:
IPs are assigned per interface — each VMInterface only receives the IPs belonging to that interface.

Source priority: 1. Static IPs from Proxmox VM config (ip= / ip6= fields per net key) 2. QEMU guest agent — network-get-interfaces queried and IPs matched to interfaces by MAC address (QEMU VMs only; not available for LXC)

VRF placement: If netbox_vrf is set on the endpoint, all synced IPs are assigned to that VRF.

Stale cleanup: IPs present in NetBox but no longer in Proxmox config are removed. This runs on every sync — even when the interface has no IPs configured, old IPs are cleaned up.

docs.title: What is NOT synced
docs.text:
• Proxmox SDN (Software-Defined Networking) zones and VNets are not synced.
  • Proxmox firewall rules are not synced.
  • VLAN tags on VM interfaces are not currently synced to NetBox.

docs.title: Storage
docs.text: Not currently synced. Proxmox storage pools and disk configuration are not synced to NetBox by this plugin.

docs.title: Current state
docs.text:
The plugin does not read Proxmox storage pool data (/api2/json/nodes/{node}/storage) or map it to NetBox objects.

VM disk sizes visible in Proxmox are not currently written to NetBox VirtualDisk objects.

docs.title: What IS synced
docs.text: The plugin syncs: - Proxmox nodes → NetBox devices - VMs and LXC containers → NetBox virtual machines - VM network interfaces and IPs → NetBox VMInterfaces and IP addresses

docs.title: Future
docs.text: Storage sync (e.g. disk size on VMs, storage pool inventory) may be considered in a future release.

docs.title: Proxmox Nodes → NetBox Devices
docs.text:
Each Proxmox cluster node is synced as a dcim.Device.

Proxmox field NetBox field Node name Device name Cluster name Cluster (virtualization) Endpoint site Device site Device type (endpoint or mapping) Device type

Device type priority: 1. Per-node mapping (ProxmoxNodeTypeMapping) — if the node name has an explicit mapping 2. Endpoint netbox_device_type — if set on the endpoint 3. Generic Proxmox Node fallback (auto-created, manufacturer: Proxmox)

docs.title: VMs and Containers → NetBox Virtual Machines
docs.text:
QEMU VMs and LXC containers are synced as virtualization.VirtualMachine.

Proxmox field NetBox field VM name Virtual machine name Node name Cluster node Cluster name Cluster vCPU count vCPUs Memory (MB) Memory

docs.title: VM Interfaces → NetBox VMInterfaces
docs.text:
Each network interface from the Proxmox VM config (net0, net1, …) is synced as a virtualization.VMInterface.

Proxmox field NetBox field Interface name Interface name MAC address Primary MAC address

docs.title: IP Addresses
docs.text:
IPs are synced per interface — each VMInterface only receives its own IPs.

Sources (in priority order): 1. Static IPs from Proxmox VM config (ip= field on net0, net1, …) 2. QEMU guest agent IPs (if guest agent is running) — matched to interface by MAC address

VRF placement: IPs are assigned to the VRF configured on the endpoint (if set).

Stale cleanup: IPs that are no longer present in Proxmox config are removed from NetBox.

docs.title: Virtual Machines
docs.text: Proxmox QEMU VMs are synced to NetBox as virtualization.VirtualMachine objects.

docs.title: What gets synced
docs.text: Proxmox field NetBox field VM name Virtual machine name Node name Cluster node Cluster name Cluster vCPU count vCPUs Memory (MB) Memory (MB) VM state Status (active / offline)

docs.title: Interfaces
docs.text:
Each network interface from the VM config (net0, net1, …) is created as a virtualization.VMInterface:

  • Interface name is derived from the Proxmox config key (e.g. net0)
  • MAC address is set as the primary_mac_address (NetBox 4.5+ MACAddress model)

docs.title: IP Addresses
docs.text:
IPs are assigned per interface — each interface only gets its own IPs.

Sources (in priority order): 1. Static IPs from Proxmox VM config (ip= field on net0, net1, …) 2. QEMU guest agent IPs — matched to the correct interface by MAC address

VRF: IPs are placed in the VRF configured on the endpoint (if set).

Stale cleanup: IPs no longer present in Proxmox config are removed from NetBox.

docs.title: Cluster placement
docs.text: VMs are associated with the NetBox Cluster that corresponds to the Proxmox cluster. If netbox_site is set on the endpoint, the cluster is scoped to that site.

docs.title: VLAN Management
docs.text: Not currently implemented. VLAN sync from Proxmox to NetBox is not part of the current plugin scope.

docs.title: Current state
docs.text:
The plugin syncs VM network interfaces and their IP addresses, but does not create or update NetBox VLAN objects based on Proxmox data.

VLAN tags configured on Proxmox VM bridge interfaces are not currently read or mapped to NetBox VLANs.

docs.title: Workaround
docs.text: VLANs should be managed directly in NetBox, either manually or via another sync tool (e.g. the UniFi sync plugin for network-level VLAN data).

docs.title: Future
docs.text: VLAN sync may be added in a future release. Track progress in GitHub Issues.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
• Installing the plugin using pip (recommended)
  • Installing the plugin using git
  • Installing the plugin using Docker

docs.title: 1. Activate NetBox virtual environment
docs.text: source /opt/netbox/venv/bin/activate

docs.title: 2. Install package
docs.text: pip install proxmox2netbox

docs.title: 3. Enable plugin
docs.text:
Edit /opt/netbox/netbox/netbox/configuration.py:

PLUGINS = ["proxmox2netbox"]

docs.title: 4. Run migrations and collect static files
docs.text:
cd /opt/netbox/netbox/
python3 manage.py migrate
python3 manage.py collectstatic --no-input

docs.title: 5. Restart NetBox
docs.text: sudo systemctl restart netbox

docs.title: 6. Configure Proxmox endpoint and run sync
docs.text:
In NetBox UI:

  • Plugins -> Proxmox2NetBox -> Endpoints -> Proxmox Endpoints
  • Create endpoint with username and either password or API token.
  • Run Sync Nodes, Sync Virtual Machines, or Full Update Sync from plugin home.

docs.title: 1. Clone repository
docs.text:
cd /opt/netbox/netbox/netbox
git clone https://github.com/patricklind/Proxmox2NetBox.git
cd Proxmox2NetBox

docs.title: 2. Activate NetBox virtual environment
docs.text: source /opt/netbox/venv/bin/activate

docs.title: 3. Install plugin package
docs.text: pip install .

docs.title: 4. Enable plugin
docs.text:
Edit /opt/netbox/netbox/netbox/configuration.py:

PLUGINS = ["proxmox2netbox"]

docs.title: 5. Run migrations and collect static files
docs.text:
cd /opt/netbox/netbox/
python3 manage.py migrate
python3 manage.py collectstatic --no-input

docs.title: 6. Restart NetBox
docs.text: sudo systemctl restart netbox

docs.title: 7. Configure Proxmox endpoint and run sync
docs.text: Use plugin UI to configure credentials and run sync operations.

docs.title: Installing the Plugin using Docker
docs.text: For netbox-docker, install this plugin through the requirements mechanism so it survives rebuilds.

docs.title: 1. Add package to local requirements
docs.text:
Add this line to your local_requirements.txt used by your deployment:

proxmox2netbox==1.2.6

docs.title: 2. Enable plugin
docs.text:
In NetBox config (plugins.py or configuration.py depending on your setup):

PLUGINS = ["proxmox2netbox"]

docs.title: 3. Rebuild/restart and run migrations
docs.text:
After container rebuild/start, run:

python3 manage.py migrate
python3 manage.py collectstatic --no-input

No external backend service is required.

docs.title: Enable HTMX Navigation on NetBox User Preferences.
docs.text:
If not enabled, this will cause visual bugs on Proxmox2NetBox GUI. For more details, check this issue.

Go to /user/preferences path and change enable HTMX navigation like below:

docs.title: From PyPI (recommended)
docs.text:
source /opt/netbox/venv/bin/activate
pip install --upgrade proxmox2netbox
cd /opt/netbox/netbox/
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py collectstatic --no-input
sudo systemctl restart netbox netbox-rq

docs.title: From git checkout (editable install)
docs.text:
cd /path/to/Proxmox2Netbox
git pull
cd /opt/netbox/netbox/
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py collectstatic --no-input
sudo systemctl restart netbox netbox-rq

docs.title: After upgrading
docs.text:
• Run python manage.py showmigrations proxmox2netbox to confirm all migrations applied.
  • Open Plugins -> Proxmox2NetBox and verify the plugin loads correctly.
  • Run a sync to verify data is still correct.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Always apply migrations after upgrading — new versions may add database columns or tables.
  • If using an editable install (pip install -e), Django will pick up code changes automatically after a service restart without reinstalling.

docs.title: Containers (Data Model)
docs.text: LXC containers are synced identically to QEMU VMs — both are represented as virtualization.VirtualMachine in NetBox.

docs.title: Fields populated by sync
docs.text: NetBox field Source name Container name from Proxmox cluster Proxmox cluster → NetBox Cluster vcpus vCPU count memory Memory in MB status active (running) / offline (stopped)

docs.title: Differences from QEMU VMs
docs.text:
• QEMU guest agent is not available for LXC containers — IP fallback via guest agent does not apply.
  • Static IPs from the LXC container network config are synced normally.

docs.title: Related objects created by sync
docs.text: NetBox model Source virtualization.VMInterface net0, net1, … from container config dcim.MACAddress MAC from net config key ipam.IPAddress Static IPs from container config

docs.title: ProxmoxEndpoint
docs.text:
Stores connection details and sync configuration for one Proxmox cluster.

Key fields: name, domain, ip_address, port, username, password, token_name, token_value, verify_ssl, netbox_site, netbox_vrf, netbox_device_type.

Managed via: Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Endpoints → Proxmox Endpoints

docs.title: ProxmoxNodeTypeMapping
docs.text:
Maps a specific Proxmox node hostname to a NetBox DeviceType.

Key fields: endpoint (FK to ProxmoxEndpoint), node_name, device_type (FK to dcim.DeviceType).

Managed via: Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Configuration → Node Type Mappings

When a sync runs, nodes are matched by name against this table. A match overrides the endpoint-level device type.

docs.title: SyncProcess
docs.text:
Records metadata about each sync execution.

Key fields: endpoint, started, completed, status.

Read-only in UI and REST API.

docs.title: NetBox objects created by sync
docs.text: NetBox model Created by dcim.Manufacturer Auto-created as Proxmox for the generic device type dcim.DeviceType Generic Proxmox Node (auto-created) or selected from endpoint/mapping dcim.DeviceRole Auto-created as Server dcim.Device One per Proxmox node virtualization.ClusterType Auto-created as Proxmox virtualization.Cluster One per Proxmox cluster virtualization.VirtualMachine One per QEMU VM or LXC container virtualization.VMInterface One per net key in VM config dcim.MACAddress One per interface with a MAC address ipam.IPAddress One per configured or agent-reported IP

docs.title: Virtual Machine (Data Model)
docs.text: Proxmox QEMU VMs and LXC containers are represented in NetBox as virtualization.VirtualMachine.

docs.title: Fields populated by sync
docs.text: NetBox field Source name VM/container name from Proxmox cluster Proxmox cluster → NetBox Cluster vcpus vCPU count memory Memory in MB status active (running) / offline (stopped)

docs.title: Related objects created by sync
docs.text: NetBox model Source virtualization.VMInterface net0, net1, … from Proxmox VM config dcim.MACAddress MAC from net config key; set as primary_mac_address ipam.IPAddress Static IPs from VM config or QEMU guest agent

docs.title: Cluster and site
docs.text:
• The Proxmox cluster is synced as a NetBox virtualization.Cluster.
  • If netbox_site is set on the endpoint, the cluster is scoped to that site (via the scope generic FK on NetBox 4.x Cluster).

docs.title: Fixed — Stale IPs not cleaned up on interfaces with no configured IPs
docs.text:
_sync_iface_ips was only called when the wanted IP set was non-empty. Interfaces that had IPs removed from Proxmox config never had their old IPs deleted from NetBox.

Fix: _sync_iface_ips is now always called — even with an empty wanted set it removes stale IPs.

docs.title: Fixed — NameError: manufacturer when endpoint has a custom device type
docs.text: manufacturer was only defined inside if device_type is None: but referenced in the return dict outside it. Sync crashed when netbox_device_type was set on the endpoint.

docs.title: Fixed — IPs assigned to wrong interface (all IPs went to eth0)
docs.text:
_extract_interface_ips returned a flat list of all IPs for a VM; all were assigned to the first interface.

Fix: Returns {net_idx: [ips]} per interface. QEMU guest agent IPs matched by MAC address via _try_agent_ips_by_mac.

docs.title: Added — Per-node Device Type Mapping UI
docs.text: New ProxmoxNodeTypeMapping model with full CRUD UI (Plugins → Proxmox2NetBox → Configuration → Node Type Mappings). Map individual node names to specific NetBox DeviceTypes.

docs.title: Fixed — Migration drift (0016), NoReverseMatch, TypeError: api_url, TemplateDoesNotExist
docs.text: Several follow-up fixes for the new model and form.

docs.title: Performance — Gunicorn cold-start
docs.text: preload_app = True added to gunicorn.py.

docs.title: Added — Per-endpoint Node Device Type
docs.text:
netbox_device_type FK on ProxmoxEndpoint. Synced nodes get the selected DeviceType (e.g. Dell PowerEdge R740) instead of always using the generic Proxmox Node.

Endpoint detail page, list table, and API serializer updated to expose the new field.

docs.title: Fixed — IPNetwork.split() crash and additional IP sync bugs
Fixed — NetBox 4.x cluster scope and VMInterface MAC address
Added — Site/VRF mapping, per-interface IP sync, guest-agent fallback
docs.text:
• netbox_site and netbox_vrf FK fields on ProxmoxEndpoint.
  • IPs from Proxmox VM config synced to correct VMInterface per interface index.
  • QEMU guest agent queried as IP fallback.

docs.title: Initial stable release
docs.text: Plugin correctly registered in NetBox. Basic sync of Proxmox nodes, QEMU VMs, and LXC containers.
